text.content:
A fortified toll checkpoint in the Midgard's Shield foothills, where the Lantern Road narrows into a series of switchbacks before climbing toward the Titan's Stair. Smoke rises from braziers on either side of a timber-and-stone gatehouse.

Public Knowledge

At a Glance

  • Type: Chainwarden fortified toll checkpoint.
  • Commander: Warden-Sergeant Eirik Greyhand.
  • Garrison: ~8 Chainwardens, rotated monthly (in theory).
  • Purpose: Tax travel in Heat and Word. Control passage between the Coastal and Interior Belts.

What You See

Cold-bitten armour, furs over mail, a timber gatehouse built across the narrowest point of the road. Braziers flank the gate; the fire is low. A ledger-desk sits under a hide awning where a clerk (when one exists) records names, cargo, and toll payments. The checkpoint has good sightlines up and down the road—approaching unseen is nearly impossible.

What It Costs

Passage through Greypass costs Heat (fuel or rations) or Word (proper travel papers, authorised permits, or a recognisable Chainwarden toll-token). Silver is accepted but worth less than warmth.

Director Notes

⚠ How to Run Greypass

Greypass is a Moderate Negotiation scene with Eirik Greyhand (Interest 2, Patience 3). The key dynamics:

  • Papers work: Presenting forged merchants' papers is a Presence test. moderate roll+ passes clean.
  • Fuel buys friendship: Offering rations or fuel earns 2 Interest immediately.
  • News earns trust: Eirik has family in Hrafnborg. Any reliable news softens him.
  • Threats are catastrophic: 8 wardens behind fortifications. −2 Patience instantly.
  • Bryn's token: One free passage, plus Eirik asks after her.

On the return journey (Beat 8), the toll-post is dark. Eirik has evacuated. A note reads: 'Recalled to Hrafnborg. Orders of the King.'

A natural vault of interlocking charred trunks deep in the Ashen Woods. The burned trees have fallen together into an accidental architecture—arches, pillars, and a vaulted 'ceiling' of blackened wood that filters grey light like stained glass made of ash.

Public Knowledge

At a Glance

  • Location: Deep in the Ashen Woods (Greenweald), east of the Interior Belt.
  • Type: Natural landmark and offering site.
  • Significance: One of the few places in the Ashen Woods where the silence feels sacred rather than dead.

What You See

The burned trunks form intersecting arches 40 feet overhead. Charcoal dust covers the floor in a fine, undisturbed layer—except around a flat stone at the centre that serves as an altar. Offerings have been left here: a carved stick, a handful of dried berries, a strip of cloth tied to a charred branch. Someone tends this place.

Director Notes

⚠ Solveig's Territory

The Charcoal Cathedral is Solveig the Burner's domain. She tends the offerings and uses the cathedral as a waypoint in her charcoal-burning routes.

  • Trigger: If the PCs leave an offering at the altar stone, Solveig shows herself. If they take offerings or damage the site, she never appears.
  • Atmosphere: Silence. Not the dead silence of the Ashen Woods, but a held silence—like a breath not yet released. The air smells of old charcoal and something faintly sweet, like tree sap long since dried.
  • Shortcut: Solveig knows a path through the cathedral's sub-structure (collapsed root tunnels) that saves time. +1 second chance (two rolls, take best) on one test in Montage Round 3.

On the return journey (Beat 8), the offering spot is empty. A charcoal stick broken in half is left: 'Either she's moved on, or she's telling us to.'

A ruined village fortified with sharpened stakes, overturned carts, and desperate ingenuity. Smoke from a single communal fire rises above the barricades. Thirty people live here—refugees, deserters, and the stubborn. They are not welcoming.

Public Knowledge

At a Glance

  • Location: On the most direct road between the Interior Belt and the Titan's Stair pass.
  • Type: Improvised refugee fortification in a ruined village.
  • Leader: Thyra Blackhand, former Chainwarden Sergeant.
  • Population: ~30 civilians, including children and elderly.

Defences

Barricades of overturned carts, sharpened stakes, and stone rubble from collapsed buildings. A single narrow gate controlled by armed sentries. Fire-pits at the perimeter—the refugees know fire slows the dead. The holdfast is defensible against small groups of draugr but will not survive a sustained assault.

Director Notes

⚠ Negotiation Scene (Beat 8)

Greyfen is a Moderate Negotiation with Thyra Blackhand (Interest 3, Patience 2). Key dynamics:

  • Hope: Sharing that the Green Heart lives, or that Kaelen bears the Ember-mark → +1 Interest.
  • Pragmatism: Tactics against draugr, fire as weapon, reinforcing barricades → +1 Interest.
  • Authority: King's name or official rank → +0 Interest, −1 Patience.
  • Threats: −2 Patience. Any attempt to force passage triggers hostility.
  • Kaelen's Mark: If Kaelen reveals her ember-mark → +2 Interest. Refugees weep. Gate opens.

Success: Gate opens, 3 days' rations, intelligence about Titan's Stair (ice, rockslide, converging dead). Thyra becomes Act 2 military contact.

Failure: Ordered away. +2 days' detour. Fighting costs 1 Victory.

Stakes: If PCs help materially, Thyra's people survive the approaching draugr column and reach Hrafnborg. If not, the holdfast falls.

The only inn in Stairwatch: a low stone building with a slate roof, a crooked chimney leaking smoke, and a sign so weathered that the painted staircase has become an abstract smear. Inside, it smells of wet wool, watered ale, and the particular sadness of dried fish.

Public Knowledge

At a Glance

  • Location: Stairwatch, at the foot of Titan's Stair.
  • Type: Waystation inn—the only lodging in town.
  • Innkeeper: Hild, a Dwarf who asks no questions and waters the ale 'just enough to be annoying.'
  • Prices: Fair. Payment in silver, barter, or useful news.

Layout

  • Common room: Long tables, a stone hearth, and benches worn smooth by generations of travellers. A hunting trophy (unidentifiable antlers) hangs over the bar.
  • Upstairs rooms: Small, cold, and clean enough. Lew Tosferd rents one 'when he bothers to sleep here.'
  • Kitchen: Hild's domain. Dried fish, hard bread, and a suspiciously good turnip stew.

Director Notes

⚠ The Inn as Social Hub

The Broken Stair is where the PCs meet Hild, get directions to Lew, and experience Stairwatch's atmosphere. Use it for:

  • Information: Hild knows everything. She won't volunteer it, but she'll trade it for courtesy.
  • Comic relief: Hild on Lew: 'The Finder? More likely at the chandler's. He owes me three nights' lodging.'
  • Grafvitnir tension: A stranger at the Bone Market asks Hild about the PCs' destination. She says nothing but warns the PCs.
  • Departure gift: If PCs helped with tasks, Hild packs extra rations: 'Bring that one-eyed bastard back alive. He owes me.'
